steps.title: Combine behavior and genetics
steps.content: The first study links breed-level behavior differences to breed-level genetic differences, especially for train ability. The second study says English mastiffs and Yorkshire terriers behave differently in train ability, so the combined findings point to a genetic difference related to that trait.

steps.title: Reject overextended claims
steps.content: Choice A predicts future genetic similarity, which the studies do not address. Choice C shifts from breed-level findings to individual dogs and adds a direction not given. Choice D says both breeds are more trainable than most breeds, which the passage never claims. The answer is B.
